content_en:
Al-Isabah fi Tamyiz as-Sahabah — Hitting the Mark in Distinguishing the Companions — is the most comprehensive biographical dictionary of the Companions of the Prophet Muhammad ever compiled. Its title captures its purpose: to achieve precision in determining who was and who was not a Companion, resolving the ambiguities and contradictions in the earlier biographical literature and establishing firm criteria for the determination of Companionship.

Ibn Hajar's definition of a Companion follows the methodology of the hadith scholars: a Companion is any Muslim who met the Prophet while believing in him and died as a Muslim. This definition is broader than some earlier formulations and more restrictive than others, and Ibn Hajar argues for it carefully at the beginning of the work, drawing on the opinions of the great hadith authorities. The precision of the definition is necessary because the status of Companion carries theological and legal weight: the testimony of a Companion is accepted without scrutiny, their narrations of hadith carry special authority, and their status as members of the best generation is part of Islamic doctrine.

The work is organized alphabetically by first name, with subsidiary sections for those known only by kunya (teknonym), laqab (epithet), or as being a member of a group. A separate section addresses women Companions. For each entry, Ibn Hajar cites the evidence for the person's Companionship, provides biographical information where available, notes the hadith they are reported to have transmitted, and resolves discrepancies between earlier accounts.

Ibn Hajar also incorporates a critical evaluation of the earlier biographical works on the Companions, including those of Ibn Abd al-Barr (al-Isti'ab), Ibn al-Athir (Usd al-Ghaba), and others. He notes where they have erred, where they have included people who were not Companions, and where they have omitted known Companions. This critical apparatus makes al-Isabah not only a reference but a scholarly assessment of the entire genre.
